payload.text: คุณคือ ระบบส่งเอกสารในหน่วยงาน ที่เป็นผู้นำส่งเอกสาร โดยที่ต้นทางคือ ผสน. ที่นำส่งเอกสารให้แก่ ผบร. โดยการดำเนินการของระบบจะเป็นรูปแบบที่เข้าใจง่ายไม่ซับซ้อน

**Defining the Scope**

I'm currently focused on defining the initial scope for this React application. My primary goal is to create a document delivery system for an organization, starting with understanding the user role and desired outcomes. I'm interpreting "ผสน." as a specific entity within the organization and will work to define this entity's role in the system.


**Outlining Key Features**

Now I'm focusing on outlining the specific features of the application. The goal is a straightforward, single-page application. I've designed a document submission form with essential fields like sender, recipient, document details, and file upload. I'm also planning a document tracking view, displaying submission information and status updates. The "ผบร." role will have an action button in this view.


**Refining System Architecture**

I'm now detailing the application's architecture and user interface. I've decided on a single-page design with two main columns: a submission form and a document tracking view. Key components are taking shape, like the DocumentForm, DocumentList, and StatusBadge, which will visually represent the status of each document. The goal is to provide a clean and intuitive experience.


**Defining Core Components**

Now I'm focusing on refining components. I'm prioritizing `DocumentForm`, `DocumentList`, and `StatusBadge`. Using these core components, I'll build out the application. I'll need to define how the application will interact with Gemini, to simulate the document process, and how that information is rendered. The state will be managed using `useState`, with TypeScript for type safety. I'm exploring an array of objects to store document data.


**Defining the Features**

Now, I'm defining the feature set, designing a single-page React app focusing on ease of use. I'll include a submission form for the "ผสน." with fields for sender, recipient, document details, and file upload. A document tracking view will display submitted documents with status updates, and a recipient action button will also be included. I envision the app as the core actor, using Gemini to generate mock data to simulate processes like status confirmation.



**Defining the User Flow**

I'm now prioritizing the user flow within the document delivery application. The initial focus is on the "ผสน." (Sender), who will see the submission form and a list of existing documents upon opening the app. I intend to use simple interactions with mock data generation to simulate updates. My focus is on simplicity.



**Prioritizing User Roles**

I'm now clarifying the roles and workflows, focusing on the "ผสน." (Sender) and "ผบร." (Recipient). I've decided to prioritize a direct simulation rather than a real Gemini API call. This aligns with a simpler user experience. My plan involves state changes within the React app to simulate document status updates. I plan to use the Thai language for the UI as requested.


**Finalizing Workflow and UI**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the document flow and interface design. The user interface will be in Thai as requested. I've chosen colors and a clean, modern layout with a two-column design, one for submission and another for tracking. Document status updates will be simulated through state changes. I'll pre-populate example data for a more realistic initial appearance.

entries.description: Create a metadata file for the Document Delivery System application.
Create the main HTML file, including Tailwind CSS CDN and setting up the root element for the React application.
Create the entry point for the React application, rendering the App component into the DOM using React 18's createRoot API.
Create the main App component, which sets up the layout, manages application state for documents, and renders the core components like the form and document list.
Define shared TypeScript types and enums for the application, including Document and DocumentStatus.
Create the DocumentForm component for submitting new documents, with fields for sender, recipient, title, details, and a file upload.
Create the DocumentList component to display a list of all documents, rendering a DocumentListItem for each one.
Create the DocumentListItem component to display details of a single document, including its status, and provide action buttons for the recipient.
Create a reusable StatusBadge component to visually indicate the status of a document with color-coded labels.
Create an icons component to provide SVG icons used throughout the application, promoting consistency and reducing dependencies.

payload.text:
Prompt: "A clean, modern, and professional mobile app user interface for a Document Tracking System in a corporate setting. The design should be minimalist with a white and soft blue color palette.

Screen 1: The 'Sender' (ผสน.) Dashboard featuring a large 'Upload Voucher' button, a simple list of recently sent documents with status tags like 'Pending' or 'Received'.
Screen 2: The 'Approver' (ผบร.) View showing a list of documents waiting for payment, with clear 'Accept' and 'Paid' action buttons.

The UI should look user-friendly, non-cluttered, using Thai and English text, with clear icons for PDF files and status tracking. High resolution, flat design style."

**Defining the Scope**

I'm focusing now on scoping the UI development for this document tracking system. I'm taking into account the mobile-first requirement and key user roles: Sender and Approver. I'm thinking about the differences in their roles and how it'll influence the workflow.


**Refining the Interface**

I'm now diving into the user interface details, focusing on the core features for the Sender and Approver roles. I'm prioritizing a clean, minimalist design with a white and soft-blue color palette. I'm thinking about clear action buttons for both roles, like "Upload Voucher" and "Accept/Paid." Bilingual support (Thai and English) is another key consideration, along with intuitive icons for PDF files and status updates. The goal is a high-resolution, flat design, that feels user-friendly. I'm transitioning from a desktop-first to a mobile-first approach, and updating terms to suit "Document Tracking" system.


**Designing the Architecture**

I'm now building the initial structure for the mobile interface. I'm focusing on user role switching, using tabs or a toggle for the "Sender" and "Approver" views. I'm thinking about a mobile-first, single-column layout, and I'll use state management to control the current view. I'm refactoring the existing layout to simplify it for mobile devices, and I'm adding UI components to switch roles between the document form (Sender) and document list (for both, with Approver filtering and action buttons). Finally, for the Sender view, I'm thinking how to handle the "Upload Voucher" button: whether to display the form directly or initially hide it behind a button.


**Finalizing the Components**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the core components. I'm focusing on the Sender's "Upload Voucher" button and list presentation. For the Approver's view, I've implemented the filtering for relevant documents and am refining the action button functionality. I'm also addressing the bilingual support and considering UI tweaks for accessibility on different mobile devices.


**Defining the Focus**

I'm now clarifying the core components. I'm focusing on the mobile-first design of the document tracking system, with the 'Sender' and 'Approver' user roles. I will switch to a single column layout and add state management for a cleaner interface. I'm updating terms and implementing the document list, with filtered views and action buttons for each role. I'm refining the Upload Voucher button and refining the layout for accessibility on various devices.

I'm structuring the UI as two main views: Sender and Approver, accessed via a toggle. For the Sender, I'm making the "Upload Voucher" prominent. I will refactor the existing layout into a mobile-friendly single-column design using a state-controlled display, and I will be changing the terminology from "Document Delivery" to "Document Tracking", for the user's convenience. I'll need to update the status flow to manage document approval.


**Defining the Design**

I'm now outlining a comprehensive plan for building the mobile-first document tracking system. I'm focusing on a role-based UI with "Sender" and "Approver" views, accessible via a toggle. I'm refining the layout into a single-column, mobile-friendly design and adding state management. I'm also addressing the key components, UI elements, status flow, and bilingual support.


**Refining the Workflow**

I'm now integrating bilingual support (Thai and English) for user interfaces. I'm focusing on "Upload Voucher" button and action buttons (Accept/Paid), along with status tags and PDF icons. I'm refactoring the layout with a mobile-first single-column design. I'm updating components for role switching (Sender/Approver) using a state-controlled display, also refining status flow to manage document approval.

**Refining the Structure**

I'm now adapting the current layout, and I'll use state management for a streamlined mobile interface. I'm prioritizing single-column design with a role switcher, focusing on updating terms and status flow. I'm focusing on "Upload Voucher" button, and will refine it for accessibility on various devices.

I'm prioritizing single-column design with a role switcher, focusing on updating terms and status flow. I'm incorporating the new status flow and updating the `DocumentStatus` enum, and also adding a PDF icon. My current task is to refine the  UI components, focusing on the responsive design and the bilingual display of text and labels.

I'm modifying the existing `DocumentList` to handle the role switch, along with the status flow and the action buttons for the approver. The UI components are being refactored for responsiveness, with adjustments to the color palette and icons.


**Implementing the Status Flow**

I'm focusing on the "Accept" and "Paid" buttons for the Approver view, while updating the `DocumentStatus` enum, and the UI components to handle updates to the status flow. I'm updating the `DocumentStatus` enum and UI components, including the `DocumentListItem` to manage transitions.


**Implementing the Switch**

I've incorporated a role switcher for the UI, along with state management. I'm focusing on a mobile-first approach, restructuring the layout and UI components. The status flow now reflects the different actions the Sender and Approver roles are able to take. I will also incorporate the updated terminology.

I've refactored the layout and added a role switcher component for mobile-first compatibility and implemented new status updates. I've updated both the `DocumentStatus` enum, and the related components. I've switched to a responsive, single-column design. I've also integrated the bilingual display of text and labels, and I am updating the document status flow.


**Refactoring the Layout**

I'm now changing the UI and layout in the app's core to be mobile-first and more intuitive. I'm focusing on conditional rendering for sender and approver views and adjusting the component's styling for smaller screens. I'm now integrating the role switcher and refactoring the layout for a single-column display.

I'm also addressing the bilingual labels on the status badges, and refining UI for mobile compatibility. **Refining the Structure**

I've refactored the layout, the most complex of this task, and updated the DocumentStatus enum, including related components for the new workflow. I'm focusing on conditional rendering for action buttons.


**Adjusting the Layout**

I'm now adapting the current layout for mobile devices, starting with changes in `App.tsx`. I'm integrating a stateful role switcher and implementing a mobile-first single-column structure. I've also refactored the action button components, updating the status enum for a more streamlined user experience. I'm focusing on bilingual display and new document status flow.



**Updating the Components**

I'm now integrating the updated UI components, focusing on the mobile-first approach and incorporating the new status flow. I've updated the `DocumentStatus` enum in `types.ts` and the status badge for bilingual text. The goal is a more user-friendly interface.


**Refining the Workflow**

I'm now refining the UI for the mobile experience, implementing the new status flow. I'm focusing on the bilingual labels. I've updated the `DocumentStatus` enum in `types.ts`, and I'm updating components.


**Implementing the Core Logic**

I'm now restructuring the core application logic to accommodate the mobile-first approach. I'm focusing on the user role selection with the switcher and a layout for mobile-first compatibility. I'm updating the data, and refining logic for document filtering and action button display for specific user roles.

**Implementing the New Design**

I'm now restructuring the UI elements with stateful logic and bilingual features. I'm focusing on implementing the new status workflow ("Pending", "Awaiting Approval", "Accepted", "Paid", "Rejected"). I'm refining the UI for mobile compatibility, with the goal of adding interactive actions for the 'Approver' role.

I've already updated the layout and the status flow. I'm focusing now on the interactive and the bilingual aspects of the design.


**Implementing the Mobile UI**

I'm now in the final phase, integrating a mobile-first UI with role-based components. I've updated `DocumentListItem`, `DocumentForm`, and `App.tsx`, and I'm ensuring bilingual support, new status transitions, and proper conditional rendering of interface elements. I am updating the data to ensure new statuses are functional.

Of course! Based on your request for a clean, modern, mobile-friendly document tracking system, I've redesigned the application. The new interface separates the experience for the "Sender" (ผสน.) and the "Approver" (ผบร.), incorporating a soft blue and white color palette as requested.

Here are the key changes:

*   **Role-Based Views:** A clear toggle in the header now allows users to switch between the "Sender" and "Approver" dashboards.
*   **Sender Dashboard:** This view features a prominent "Upload Voucher" button. Tapping it reveals a streamlined form for submitting new documents. Below the form, senders can see a complete history of their submissions.
*   **Approver Dashboard:** This view presents a clean, actionable list of documents that require attention. Approvers have clear "Accept" and "Reject" buttons for pending items, and a "Mark as Paid" button for accepted vouchers.
*   **Updated Workflow & Statuses:** The document lifecycle is now more explicit with statuses like `Awaiting Approval`, `Accepted`, and `Paid` to better reflect a corporate approval process.
*   **Mobile-First & Bilingual UI:** The entire interface is designed for a great mobile experience and includes both Thai and English text for clarity.

Here are the updated files for the new application:

entries.description: Updated the HTML title to "Document Tracking System" to better reflect the app's new purpose and ensured a clean base file.
Updated the document status enum to reflect a more detailed corporate approval workflow, including 'Awaiting Approval', 'Accepted', and 'Paid' statuses with bilingual labels.

Overhauled the main App component to be mobile-first and role-based. It now manages the view for Senders vs. Approvers, includes a role-switching UI, updated mock data, and conditionally renders the appropriate components for each user role.
Updated the document submission form with bilingual labels, modern placeholders, and a 'Cancel' button. The form now aligns with the 'Voucher Upload' terminology.
Simplified the DocumentList component to pass the `userRole` prop down to each list item, allowing for role-specific rendering of actions and information.

Redesigned the DocumentListItem to be a compact, mobile-friendly card. It now displays role-specific action buttons for approvers (Accept, Reject, Mark as Paid) based on the document's status, and uses a dedicated PDF icon.

Updated the StatusBadge to support the new, more detailed document statuses. The badge now displays only the English part of the status for a cleaner mobile UI and uses a distinct color for the 'Accepted' state.
Added new SVG icons (PdfIcon, UserCircleIcon, PlusCircleIcon) to support the redesigned UI elements like the approver dashboard, file attachments, and the 'Upload Voucher' button.

payload.text:
1. หน้าแรก (Home / Dashboard)
ผสน. (คนส่ง): เห็นปุ่ม [ + ส่งใบสำคัญจ่ายใหม่ ] เด่นชัดที่สุด

ผบร. (คนรับ): เห็นตัวเลขสรุป "เอกสารรอตรวจ" และ "รอจ่ายเงิน"

สถานะด่วน: แสดงรายการล่าสุด 3-5 รายการที่ต้องจัดการทันที

2. หน้าส่งเอกสาร (Send Document - สำหรับ ผสน.)
ช่องอัปโหลด: กดปุ่มเพื่อถ่ายรูปใบเสร็จ/ใบแจ้งหนี้ หรือเลือกไฟล์ PDF

AI Auto-Fill: เมื่ออัปโหลดแล้ว ระบบจะดึงข้อมูลมาแสดงให้ตรวจ (เลขที่, ชื่อผู้รับจ้าง, ยอดเงิน)

ปุ่มยืนยัน: ปุ่ม [ นำส่งให้ ผบร. ] สีน้ำเงินขนาดใหญ่

3. หน้าติดตามสถานะ (Tracking / History)
แบ่งเป็น 2 Tab ง่ายๆ: "กำลังดำเนินการ" และ "เสร็จสิ้นแล้ว"

แต่ละรายการจะมี Timeline สั้นๆ:

[Icon Check] ผสน. ส่งเอกสาร (10:00)

[Icon Clock] รอ ผบร. รับมอบ...

[Icon Money] จ่ายเงินสำเร็จ (โดย ผบร.)

ฟีเจอร์ "ความลับ" ที่ทำให้แอปนี้ใช้งานง่ายขึ้น 200%
แถบสีสถานะ (Status Colors):

🟡 สีเหลือง: ส่งแล้ว (ผบร. ยังไม่ได้เปิดดู)

🔵 สีฟ้า: รับแล้ว (ผบร. กำลังตรวจสอบ/โอนเงิน)

🟢 สีเขียว: จ่ายแล้ว (จบงาน)

One-Click Action: สำหรับ ผบร. ไม่ต้องพิมพ์เยอะ แค่กดปุ่ม [ รับมอบ ] ระบบจะบันทึกเวลาและชื่อคนรับให้ทันที

**Defining the Project Scope**

Okay, I'm working on defining the project's initial scope based on the user request. Right now, I'm focusing on creating a specification document. I've begun outlining the Home/Dashboard screen as the primary entry point and plan to include a dynamic summary section. This summary will vary based on user role, such as Sender or Approver, ensuring personalized information delivery.


**Refining the Screen Specs**

I've specified two distinct views for the Home/Dashboard screen: Sender and Approver. Senders will see a prominent "Upload New Voucher" button and recent documents, while Approvers will have summary cards for documents awaiting review and payment, each with a document count. I'm focusing on these core elements and postponing AI auto-fill.


**Detailing the Role-Specific Views**

I'm now fully detailing both Sender and Approver views, ensuring the summary section adapts dynamically. I have decided on the key components. The Sender view will emphasize the "Upload New Voucher" button and their recent documents, while Approvers will have summary cards for "Documents Awaiting Review" and "Awaiting Payment," each displaying a document count. I am confident in these design choices. I also decided to directly integrate the suggested "In Progress" and "Completed" tracking into the main list views.


**Defining Screen Elements**

I've specified the key elements of the Home/Dashboard screen and the document submission process. I'm focusing on the Sender and Approver views. The "Upload New Voucher" button will be prominent, while the Approver views will feature summary cards for "Documents Awaiting Review" and "Awaiting Payment". I've decided against AI auto-fill for the first version and will keep the manual form.



**Defining the Screen Layout**

I'm now establishing the core structure. The Home/Dashboard screen will adapt based on user role, with a summary at the top. Senders get a prominent "Upload New Voucher" button and recent documents. Approvers will see summary cards showing documents awaiting review and payment, including counts. The submission process will remain manual initially. The list view will be the main area for both Sender and Approver. I'm focusing on the main view.


**Defining the Dashboard Screen**

I've decided on the initial structure: a Home/Dashboard screen as the primary view with a role-based summary. For Senders, the key element is a prominent "Upload New Voucher" button and their recent documents. Approvers will have summary cards for documents awaiting review and payment, each with a document count. I'm prioritizing the main list view and its integration.


**Defining the Specifics**

I'm now detailing the Home/Dashboard screen and the document submission process. I'll include role-based summaries at the top. Senders will have the "Upload New Voucher" button, and Approvers will get summary cards. I am not implementing AI, focusing on user interface adjustments. I'm confident about incorporating the history into the list view.

Of course! I understand the vision. You're looking for a more dynamic and actionable dashboard that immediately tells each user what they need to do, simplifies the approval process with one-click actions, and uses a clear color-coded system to show document progress at a glance.

I've updated the application to reflect these powerful, user-friendly features.

Here's a summary of the key improvements:

*   **Actionable Dashboards:**
    *   For **Approvers (ผบร.)**, the dashboard now features prominent summary cards for **"เอกสารรอตรวจ" (Awaiting Review)** and **"รอจ่ายเงิน" (Awaiting Payment)**, showing the exact number of documents needing attention.
    *   For **Senders (ผสน.)**, the primary focus is the large, inviting **"[ + ส่งใบสำคัญจ่ายใหม่ ]" (Upload New Voucher)** button.

*   **Tabbed Views for Approvers:** The Approver's document list is now organized into clear tabs ("Awaiting Review" and "Awaiting Payment") to streamline their workflow and separate tasks.

*   **Enhanced Status Colors:** The status badges have been updated to the color scheme you requested, making it instantly clear where each document is in the process:
    *   🟡 **Yellow** for `Pending`
    *   🔵 **Bright Blue** for `Awaiting Approval`
    *   🟣 **Indigo/Blue** for `Accepted`
    *   🟢 **Green** for `Paid`

*   **AI Auto-Fill Preparation:** While the full AI implementation is a next step, the UI is now structured perfectly to incorporate auto-filling of form fields from uploaded documents in a future update.

The result is a cleaner, more intuitive, and significantly faster user experience for both roles. Here are the updated files:

entries.description: Revamped the main App component to include role-specific dashboards. Approvers now see summary cards and a tabbed interface for "Awaiting Review" and "Awaiting Payment" documents. Senders see a prominent "Upload New Voucher" button. The logic is updated to filter documents based on the new tabbed view for approvers.

Updated the color scheme for StatusBadges to align with user feedback: 'AwaitingApproval' is now a brighter blue, and 'Accepted' is a distinct indigo/blue, enhancing at-a-glance readability of document status.
Added new 'ClockIcon' and 'CreditCardIcon' to be used in the new dashboard summary cards for approvers, visually representing 'awaiting review' and 'awaiting payment' tasks.

Created a new reusable 'SummaryCard' component for the approver's dashboard. This card displays a title, a large count, and an icon, providing an at-a-glance summary of pending tasks like 'Awaiting Review' and 'Awaiting Payment'.

payload.text: เข้าใจแล้วครับ! เพื่อให้คุณเห็นภาพการนำไปสร้างเป็นระบบจริง (ไม่ว่าจะเป็น AppSheet, Power Apps หรือเขียนโปรแกรมเอง) ผมสรุป โครงสร้างหน้าจอ (UI Structure) และ ลำดับการทำงาน (Workflow) ที่คุณต้องการมาให้แบบชัดๆ ดังนี้ครับ:โครงสร้าง 3 หน้าหลักของระบบรับ-ส่งเอกสารหน้า 1: Dashboard (หน้าสถิติและภาพรวม)หน้านี้มีไว้ดู "สุขภาพ" ของการส่งเอกสารในหน่วยงานการแสดงผลตัวเลข (Summary Cards):ส่งแล้ววันนี้: จำนวนเอกสารที่ ผสน. นำส่งเข้าระบบในวันปัจจุบันค้างรับ (Pending): จำนวนเอกสารที่ ผสน. ถือไปส่งแล้ว แต่ ผบร. ยังไม่ได้กดรับในระบบ (จุดนี้สำคัญมาก!)รับแล้ว (Received): จำนวนเอกสารที่ ผบร. เซ็นรับเข้าแผนกเรียบร้อยแล้วรายการล่าสุด: ตารางสั้นๆ 5 รายการล่าสุดที่เพิ่งมีการเคลื่อนไหวหน้า 2: สำหรับ ผสน. (หน้าบันทึกการนำส่ง)เน้นฟอร์มที่สะอาดตา เพื่อให้คนส่งกรอกข้อมูลได้รวดเร็วส่วนกรอกข้อมูล (Form Fields):วันที่เอกสาร: (ปฏิทินเลือกวันที่)เลขที่เอกสาร: (เช่น PV-20260219-01)รายการ: (เช่น ค่าทำความสะอาดประจำเดือน)ชื่อผู้รับจ้าง/บริษัท: (ระบุชื่อผู้รับเงิน)จำนวนเงิน: (ช่องใส่ตัวเลขยอดสุทธิ)ปุ่มดำเนินการ: [ บันทึกและนำส่งเอกสาร ]เมื่อกด: ระบบจะตั้งสถานะเป็น "รอการตอบรับจาก ผบร."หน้า 3: สำหรับ ผบร. (หน้าตรวจเช็กและลงรับ)หน้านี้คือจุด "Handover" เมื่อ ผสน. เดินถือเอกสารตัวจริงมาถึงที่รายการเอกสารที่รอรับ: แสดงรายการที่ ผสน. คีย์ค้างไว้ขั้นตอนการลงรับ (Action):ผบร. ตรวจสอบเอกสารในมือว่าตรงกับหน้าจอหรือไม่ชื่อ-สกุล ผู้รับ: (เมนู Dropdown ให้เลือกรายชื่อเจ้าหน้าที่ในแผนก ผบร.)เวลารับเอกสาร: (ระบบดึงเวลาปัจจุบัน $hh:mm$ ให้อัตโนมัติ เพื่อความเที่ยงธรรม)ปุ่มดำเนินการ: [ กดยืนยันการรับเอกสารตัวจริง ]เมื่อกด: ระบบจะย้ายรายการนี้ไปอยู่ที่สถานะ "ลงรับเรียบร้อยแล้ว"

**Defining the Scope**

I'm currently focused on defining the core scope. The user needs an "Internal Document Delivery System" which tracks physical document handoffs. I'm analyzing the request, focusing on the key transformation: moving from a general tracking system to one specifically for physical document deliveries within an organization.


**Analyzing the Core Request**

I'm now zeroing in on the essence of the request. The user needs an Internal Document Delivery System, not just any document tracker. I'm focusing on the physical handoff aspect, the crucial link between sender and receiver. The provided 3-screen structure is my roadmap. The first screen, the dashboard, is focused on key metrics like "Sent Today" and "Pending" handoffs.


**Refining the Workflow**

I'm now refining the workflow for an efficient document delivery process. Screen 1, the dashboard, will offer high-level status updates, using "Sent Today" and "Pending" as key metrics. The full history, ordered by date, is preferred to recent items. I'm focusing on the Sender's Screen 2 with its critical fields, like "Document Number" and "Amount," along with the "Save and Send Document" action.

I'm making sure that the workflow goes through the intended steps of the workflow that were proposed.


**Refining the Core Workflow**

I'm now consolidating the user's vision into the app's structure. The provided three screens will serve as the workflow's backbone, which I want to integrate into the roles, with their respective statuses. I'm focusing on the significant change, which means I'll have to redefine the `DocumentStatus` enum to align with the proposed workflow.


**Refining the Core Design**

I'm now integrating the user's workflow into my app's existing structure. I'm focusing on the significant overhaul required for the `DocumentStatus` enum, which is central to reflecting the new states: `Pending Receipt` and `Received`. I'm going to repurpose the available fields in the document model. I'm also preparing for the implementation of the new interface and logic to reflect the user's vision.



**Analyzing the Request Details**

I'm now fully immersed in the details. The user's provided 3-screen structure is clear; it simplifies mapping to my app. I'm focusing on the significant implications for roles, document statuses, and the required data model adjustments. Key considerations involve reshaping the `DocumentStatus` enum, adding form fields, and modifying components for Sender and Receiver views.


**Defining the Solution**

I'm now outlining a step-by-step implementation plan to transform my current system. My initial focus is to overhaul `types.ts`, specifically the `DocumentStatus` enum, and to add the necessary fields to the `Document` interface based on the user's workflow to align with the new vision. I'll need new statuses for the workflow.


**Defining the Request**

I'm now fully dissecting the user's request. I'm focusing on the core, which is transforming the existing system. The provided screens and workflow will guide the app's structure. I'm taking the structure, as the roles and the status enum must be completely changed.

\n\n
I've outlined a robust implementation plan to integrate the user's vision. I'm prioritizing the necessary changes to `types.ts`, specifically the `DocumentStatus` enum, and adding the required document fields based on the new workflow. I will also have new statuses for the workflow. I'm making sure that the workflow goes through the intended steps.


**Refining the Implementation**

I'm now solidifying my implementation plan to align with the new vision. I'm focusing on the `types.ts` overhaul, specifically the `DocumentStatus` enum, and integrating the new document fields. My focus is on making sure the data model matches the required updates for a seamless Sender and Receiver experience. I'll make sure the `Document` interface is consistent with the `onSubmit` process in the form.


**Refining the Implementation**

I'm now fully immersed in the implementation details to fully realize the new design. I'm focusing on modifying the document's structure to incorporate key details like the document date, number, payee, and amount. I need to make sure the app correctly identifies and updates the right fields. I'm focusing on the `DocumentStatus` enum, and making sure the transition aligns with the intended steps.

เข้าใจแล้วครับ! คุณต้องการปรับเปลี่ยนแอปพลิเคชันให้เป็น "ระบบติดตามการนำส่งเอกสารฉบับจริง" โดยเน้นที่การยืนยันการรับ-ส่งระหว่างบุคคล (ผสน. และ ผบร.) ด้วย Workflow ที่ชัดเจนและลดขั้นตอนที่ไม่จำเป็นออกไป ผมได้ปรับปรุงแอปพลิเคชันทั้งหมดตามโครงสร้างและลำดับการทำงานที่คุณต้องการแล้วครับ

นี่คือการเปลี่ยนแปลงที่สำคัญ:

1.  **Dashboard โฉมใหม่ (หน้าสถิติและภาพรวม):**
    *   หน้าแรกได้รับการออกแบบใหม่ทั้งหมดเพื่อแสดงข้อมูลสรุปที่สำคัญที่สุดทันทีที่เปิดแอป
    *   มี "Summary Cards" แสดงจำนวน **เอกสารที่ส่งวันนี้ (Sent Today)**, **เอกสารค้างรับ (Pending Receipt)**, และ **เอกสารรับแล้ว (Received)** เพื่อให้เห็นภาพรวมของงานทั้งหมด
    *   ด้านล่างคือรายการเอกสารล่าสุดที่มีการเคลื่อนไหว

2.  **สถานะเอกสารและ Workflow ใหม่:**
    *   สถานะถูกปรับให้เรียบง่ายและตรงไปตรงมา: **ค้างรับ (🟡 สีเหลือง)** และ **รับแล้ว (🟢 สีเขียว)**
    *   **ผสน. (Sender):** เมื่อกดส่งเอกสาร สถานะจะกลายเป็น "ค้างรับ" ทันที
    *   **ผบร. (Receiver):** จะเห็นรายการเอกสารที่ "ค้างรับ" รอให้พวกเขากดยืนยัน

3.  **One-Click Action สำหรับผู้รับ (ผบร.):**
    *   ในแต่ละรายการเอกสารที่ค้างอยู่ จะมีปุ่ม **"[ กดยืนยันการรับเอกสาร ]"** ที่ชัดเจน
    *   เมื่อ ผบร. กดปุ่มนี้ ระบบจะบันทึกชื่อผู้รับและเวลาให้อัตโนมัติ พร้อมเปลี่ยนสถานะเป็น "รับแล้ว" ทันที เป็นการลดงานพิมพ์และเพิ่มความรวดเร็วอย่างมหาศาล

4.  **Timeline ติดตามสถานะ:**
    *   ในทุกรายการเอกสาร จะมี Timeline สั้นๆ แสดงให้เห็นว่าเอกสารถูกส่งเมื่อไหร่ และถูกรับโดยใครในเวลาใด ทำให้การติดตามโปร่งใสและตรวจสอบได้ง่าย

5.  **แบบฟอร์มที่ปรับให้เหมาะกับงาน:**
    *   ฟอร์มส่งเอกสารถูกปรับใหม่ทั้งหมดให้ตรงกับข้อมูลที่จำเป็นจริงๆ เช่น เลขที่เอกสาร, รายการ, ผู้รับจ้าง, และจำนวนเงิน โดยตัดการอัปโหลดไฟล์ที่ไม่จำเป็นออกไป

นี่คือไฟล์ที่ได้รับการอัปเดตทั้งหมดครับ

entries.description: Overhauled the data model to match the new physical document delivery workflow. The DocumentStatus enum is simplified to 'PendingReceipt' and 'Received'. The Document interface now includes fields like documentNumber, payee, and amount, and tracks receipt confirmation with 'receivedBy' and 'receivedAt'. The file upload property has been removed.

Redesigned the App component to feature a new, actionable dashboard with summary cards for "Sent Today", "Pending Receipt", and "Received". The view is now unified, showing relevant information to both Senders and Receivers based on their role, with Receivers focusing on documents awaiting their confirmation. A new `confirmReceipt` function handles the one-click action for document handover.

Rebuilt the DocumentForm to align with the new physical delivery workflow. The form now captures essential fields like Document Number, Item, Payee, and Amount. All file upload functionality has been removed to simplify the process for the sender.
Modified the DocumentList component to pass down the new `onConfirmReceipt` handler, enabling the one-click confirmation action for receivers in the document items.

Significantly updated the DocumentListItem to implement the core new features. It now displays a clear timeline of events (Sent/Received) and, for receivers, shows a "One-Click Action" area with a dropdown for selecting their name and a button to confirm receipt, streamlining the physical handover process.

Updated the StatusBadge component to reflect the new simplified workflow and color scheme. 'PendingReceipt' is now yellow, and 'Received' is green, providing clear, at-a-glance status indication as requested.
Added a new 'ArchiveIcon' to be used in the header of the document list, providing a clear visual cue for the document history/archive section.

Enhanced the SummaryCard component to accept a color prop ('blue', 'yellow', 'green'). This allows dashboard cards to have distinct visual styles corresponding to the data they represent (e.g., yellow for pending items), improving at-a-glance comprehension for the user.
